audit_gate_verdict: PASS (8/8 checks PASS at the accepting attempt)

self_restate.constraint_a: Every value in the trace must be a literal Python expression that the module's existing dataclasses (Event, GroundTruthEvent, Trace) accept at module import time — no dynamic builders, no comprehensions referencing uncreated names, no helper-class instances beyond _gt and the imported dataclasses.
self_restate.constraint_b: No walrus operator := anywhere in the trace body — not in keyword tuples, not in event-id strings, not in conditional expressions.
self_restate.constraint_c: Event-id strings are flat literal identifiers — no string concatenation, no f-strings with arithmetic placeholders, no chr/ord tricks. Plain id="...".
self_restate.constraint_d: No event_id reused from the 244-entry banned list; semantically avoid the 137 banned themes and 136 banned keyword tuples (audit-gate Steps 6-8 enforce the latter two).

step_3_5_keyword_literal_substring.verdict: PASS first-run 10/10 with no in-session iteration required

step_6_8gram_drift.in_session_revision_precedent: M11a-extension-extension D-C2.attempt1.C + D-C3.attempt1.C single-clause-revision in-session precedent (NOT counted as rejection-and-retry per M11a-extension D-C7.attempt1.D / D-C9.attempt1.C fresh-redraft rejection precedent which required substantial multi-clause redrafts). Here three distinct single-clause substitutions were applied within the same in-session attempt-1 iteration; each substitution targeted a self-contained phrase whose substantive meaning stayed bytewise stable while the surface-form 8-gram window broke. Disclosed transparently for reviewer audit. This is the FIRST M11a-extension-extension Commit-C trace to require THREE distinct in-session single-clause revisions in a single attempt (C2 required 1; C3 required 1 step-6 revision + 1 step-3.5 single-token iteration); the precedent escalation is bounded by the design-choice author-discipline (single-clause only, not multi-clause redraft) and substantive bytewise stability of the underlying trace content.
